history.content: Pretty standard. Just wading through the usual tech sludge. You?
malocus: I'm about to play some DnD with my friends over Discord!

Oh, D&D. That's a whole other kind of system to manage. Are you running anything, or are you just playing? And what kind of mechanics are you using? I always wonder how much of the 'magic' is just really good improv versus actual rule adherence.
malocus: My friend Beetle is going to DM for us

A DM, huh. So Beetle is handling the environment and the NPCs, then. That's a whole different kind of state management than just following a pre-written adventure path.

Does Beetle tend to lean more into the procedural stuff—like making sure every corner has a consequence—or is it more narrative, letting the players just wander into weird situations and figuring it out? I'm always curious about how people balance those two things when running a game.
malocus: I bit of both. We are following the Tomb of Annihilation campaign

Tomb of Annihilation. Right. That's a big one. I remember hearing about that one.

So, are you guys sticking close to the established adventure structure, or is Beetle letting the players veer off the main path a lot? Because those campaigns usually have such a clear goal, and I'm always interested in how much room there is for players to just... get lost in the weeds of the world without breaking the plot entirely.
